Description: PHP recompiles scripts on every request without OPcache wasting CPU cycles
Do: Enable OPcache in production. Set opcache.validate_timestamps=0 for max performance
Dont: Leave OPcache disabled or use default settings in production
Code Good:
; php.ini production
opcache.enable=1
opcache.memory_consumption=256
opcache.max_accelerated_files=20000
opcache.validate_timestamps=0
opcache.revalidate_freq=0
opcache.preload=/var/www/preload.php
opcache.preload_user=www-data
Code Bad: ; php.ini (default)\nopcache.enable=0\n; or not configured at all
Measurement: 10-50x faster script execution; check with opcache_get_status()

Description: PHP 8.0+ JIT compiler provides additional performance for CPU-intensive code
Do: Enable JIT for CPU-heavy operations (math, image processing). Tracing mode for general use
Dont: Expect JIT to speed up I/O-bound code (database queries, HTTP calls)
Code Good:
; php.ini
opcache.jit=tracing
opcache.jit_buffer_size=128M

; Or for function-level JIT:
opcache.jit=function
Code Bad: opcache.jit=off\n; or not using PHP 8.0+
Measurement: 5-30% improvement on CPU-bound tasks; minimal impact on I/O-bound

Description: Loading related models in a loop triggers N+1 queries. Use eager loading to batch queries
Do: Use with() for eager loading. Use preventLazyLoading() in development to catch N+1 early
Dont: Load relationships inside loops or rely on implicit lazy loading
Measurement: Use Laravel Debugbar or Telescope to count queries per request

Description: Queries on unindexed columns cause full table scans degrading performance exponentially with data size
Do: Add indexes to columns used in WHERE, JOIN, ORDER BY. Use composite indexes for multi-column queries
Dont: Add indexes on every column or ignore them entirely
Measurement: Use EXPLAIN to analyze query plans; monitor slow query log

Description: Loading thousands of records at once exhausts PHP memory limits
Do: Use chunk() cursor() or lazy() for processing large datasets
Dont: Call all() or get() on tables with millions of rows

Description: Repeatedly running expensive queries when results rarely change wastes database resources
Do: Cache expensive queries with remember(). Use cache tags for group invalidation
Dont: Cache everything with no invalidation strategy causing stale data
Measurement: Use Debugbar to identify repeated queries; cache hit ratio monitoring

Description: Laravel recompiles routes and config on every request in development mode
Do: Run php artisan optimize in production to cache routes config events views
Dont: Forget to cache routes and config in production deployment
Code Good:
# Production deployment script:
php artisan config:cache
php artisan route:cache
php artisan view:cache
php artisan event:cache
# Or all at once:
php artisan optimize
Code Bad:
# No caching in production:
# Routes parsed from files on every request
# Config arrays rebuilt from .env on every request
# 50-100ms overhead per request
Measurement: Compare response times with and without artisan optimize

Description: Concatenating strings in loops creates many intermediate strings consuming memory
Do: Use array + implode or output buffering for building strings in loops
Dont: Concatenate with .= in large loops
column12: Profile with memory_get_usage() before and after

Description: Serializing entire Eloquent models with relationships for queues or sessions wastes resources
Do: Serialize only IDs or minimal data. Fetch fresh models in job handlers
Dont: Serialize full model instances with all relationships into queue payloads
Measurement: Check queue payload sizes in failed_jobs table

Description: Creating new HTTP client instances per request wastes TCP connections
Do: Reuse HTTP client instances. Use persistent connections. Configure connection pools
Dont: Create new Guzzle client per API call
Code Bad: true);\n    }\n}"
column12: Monitor connection count and TCP handshake overhead

Description: SELECT * fetches all columns including BLOBs and large text fields wasting memory and bandwidth
Do: Use select() to fetch only needed columns. Use pluck() for single column values
Dont: Always use get() or all() which fetches every column,// Only fetch what you need:\n$users = User::select('id'
Code Good: 'name'
Code Bad: 'email')->where('active'
column13: // Fetches ALL columns including avatar_blob, bio_text, etc.\n$users = User::where('active', true)->get();\n// Only using $user->name in view, but loaded everything
column15: Compare query execution time and memory usage

Description: Using Eloquent for bulk operations is dramatically slower than raw queries
Do: Use insert() for bulk inserts. Use DB::statement() for bulk updates. Use upsert() for upsert
Dont: Loop through records calling save() or create() individually for bulk operations

Description: Composer PSR-4 autoloading scans directories at runtime. Classmap is faster for production
Do: Run composer dump-autoload --optimize (-o) in production to generate classmap
Dont: Use default PSR-4 autoloading in production without optimization
Code Good:
# Production deployment:
composer install --no-dev --optimize-autoloader
# Or:
composer dump-autoload --optimize --classmap-authoritative

# In composer.json:
\config": {
    "optimize-autoloader": true
Code Bad: \n    \"classmap-authoritative\": true\n}"
Impact: # Development autoloading in production:\ncomposer install\n# PSR-4 hits filesystem on every class resolution
column12: Compare autoloader overhead with and without optimization

Description: Model boot methods and observers fire on every model instantiation adding overhead
Do: Limit model boot operations. Disable events for bulk operations. Use withoutEvents for imports
Dont: Register expensive operations in model boot that run on every single model creation/load
Measurement: Profile model instantiation time with Debugbar

Description: Unoptimized Blade views with many includes and unscoped partials slow down rendering
Do: Cache compiled views. Minimize nested includes. Use fragments and lazy loading components
Code Bad: Medium
Impact: Measure view render time with Debugbar Timing tab

Description: File-based sessions don't scale with multiple servers and cause locking issues
Do: Use Redis or database for sessions in production. Configure session lifetime appropriately
Dont: Use file session driver in production with multiple app servers
Code Good:
// .env production:
SESSION_DRIVER=redis
SESSION_LIFETIME=120
SESSION_SECURE_COOKIE=true

// config/session.php
'driver' => env('SESSION_DRIVER', 'redis'),
'connection' => 'session', // dedicated Redis connection
Measurement: Monitor session read/write times; check for session lock contention

Description: Default PHP-FPM settings are for development. Production needs tuned worker counts
Do: Calculate workers based on available RAM. Use pm=static for predictable load
Dont: Use default dynamic PM settings with too few workers for production load
Code Good:
[www]
pm = static
pm.max_children = 50
pm.max_requests = 500
request_terminate_timeout = 60s

; Formula: max_children = (Total RAM - System RAM) / Avg PHP Process RAM
; Example: (8GB - 2GB) / 40MB = ~150 workers

; Monitor:
pm.status_path = /fpm-status
Code Bad:
[www]
pm = dynamic
pm.max_children = 5
pm.start_servers = 2
pm.min_spare_servers = 1
pm.max_spare_servers = 3
; Default dev settings, can't handle 50+ concurrent requests
Measurement: Monitor with pm.status_path; check for max_children reached

Description: Processing queue jobs one at a time limits throughput for parallelizable work
Do: Use Laravel Horizon for Redis queues. Batch independent jobs. Chain dependent jobs
Dont: Run single queue worker expecting it to handle high throughput
Code Bad:
# Single worker:
php artisan queue:work
# Processes one job at a time
# No monitoring, no auto-restart
Measurement: Use Horizon dashboard; monitor jobs/second and wait times

Description: Loading all records to paginate in PHP wastes memory and database resources
Do: Use database-level pagination. Use cursor pagination for large datasets (no count query)
Dont: Load all records then slice with array_slice() or collection methods
Code Bad: $allUsers = User::all(); // Loads 100k records\n$page = array_slice($allUsers->toArray(), ($page - 1) * 20, 20);\n// Out of memory with large tables
Measurement: Compare paginate() vs all() memory usage on large tables

Description: Uncompressed API responses transfer more data increasing latency
Do: Enable gzip/brotli compression at Nginx level. Use resource collections for consistent output
Dont: Serve uncompressed JSON responses or compress in PHP
Code Bad: # No compression configured\n# JSON responses sent as-is\n# 100KB response could be 15KB with gzip
Measurement: Compare response sizes with/without compression in DevTools
